---
title: "TCBL Models — Interactive Mind Map"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    minimal: true
---

::: {.column-page}

```{=html}
<style>
  body { margin: 0; padding: 0; }
  #mindmap-container { 
    width: 100vw; 
    height: 90vh; 
  }
  .controls {
    padding: 10px 20px;
    background: #f5f5f5;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;
    display: flex;
    gap: 10px;
    align-items: center;
  }
  .controls button {
    padding: 6px 16px;
    border: 1px solid #ccc;
    border-radius: 4px;
    background: white;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .controls button:hover { background: #e8e8e8; }
  .back-link { 
    margin-left: auto; 
    color: #0066cc; 
    text-decoration: none; 
  }
</style>

<div class="controls">
  <strong>TCBL Model Taxonomy</strong>
  <button onclick="mm.fit()">🔄 Fit</button>
  <button onclick="mm.rescale(1.25)">➕ Zoom In</button>
  <button onclick="mm.rescale(0.8)">➖ Zoom Out</button>
  <a href="index.html" class="back-link">← Back to Article</a>
</div>

<svg id="mindmap-container"></svg>

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/markmap-view@0.17"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/markmap-lib@0.17"></script>

<script>
const md = `
# TCBL Models

## Full-physics Models
### WRF (Weather Research & Forecasting)
#### WRF-ARW
- Turbulence parameterizations (YSU, MYJ)
- Bottom boundary conditions
- Initial conditions & data assimilation
- Ensemble prediction
#### HWRF (Hurricane WRF)
- Operational forecasting
- Ocean coupling
#### HWCM (Hybrid WRF Cyclone Model)
### Other Models
- GFS (Global Forecast System)
- CM1 (Cloud Model 1 / LES)

## Diagnostic Models
### Axisymmetric (Column) Models
#### Hybrid models (Shapiro 1983)
#### Height-averaged models (Chow 1971)
#### Nonlinear analytical (Kuo 1982)
#### Linear analytical (Kepert 2001)
### Parametric — Empirical
#### Gradient Profiles
- **Holland (1980)**: p = pc + Δp·exp(-A/r^B)
- **Willoughby (2006)**: Piecewise with transition
- **Emanuel (2004)**: Potential intensity theory
- Asymmetric profiles (Xie 2006, Mattocks 2008)
#### TC Track Models
- Circular sub-region method
- Full-track method (Vickery 2000)
#### Vertical Wind Profiles
- Surface Wind Reduction Factor (SWRF)
- TCBL profile models
### Parametric — Analytical
#### K01 (Kepert 2001) — Linear
#### Column linear model (Meng 1995)
#### Recent developments
### 2D-slab Model
- Height-averaged primitive equations
- Surrogates for PBWE
- Empirical corrections needed
### 2D Hybrid Models
- Between 2D and 3D
- Prescribed vertical profiles
### **3D Nonlinear — KW01** ⭐
#### KW01 model (Kepert & Wang 2001)
- Most theoretically rigorous
- Full nonlinear primitive equations
- Captures low-level jet (LLJ)
#### Y08 model (Yoshizumi 2008)
#### Linearization for efficiency
#### Extensive validation
#### Physics
- Turbulence closure (M-Y, Louis)
- Vertical advection (~50% LLJ effect)
- Landfall & land-sea contrast
- Thermodynamic coupling
#### Comparison to other models
- vs 2D-slab (excessive asymmetry)
- vs Parametric (limited physics)
- vs Full-physics (CM1, WRF-ARW)

## Data-driven Methods
### Modifying radial profiles
### Introducing asymmetries
### POD-based models
- TARGET(t) = MFLD + Σ Wi(t)·PCi

## NatHaz Investigation
### GPU-accelerated MC simulation
### Turbulence closure optimization
### Surface wind analysis
### Future: ML integration
`;

const { Transformer } = window.markmap;
const transformer = new Transformer();
const { root } = transformer.transform(md);

const { Markmap } = window.markmap;
const mm = Markmap.create('#mindmap-container', {
  autoFit: true,
  duration: 500,
  maxWidth: 300,
  color: (node) => {
    const depth = node.state?.depth || 0;
    const colors = ['#003366', '#0066cc', '#3399ff', '#66b3ff', '#99ccff', '#cce5ff'];
    return colors[Math.min(depth, colors.length - 1)];
  }
}, root);

setTimeout(() => mm.fit(), 500);
</script>
```

:::
